skill.name: memory-systems
skill.description: Design memory architectures for AI agents using working memory, persistent storage, vector retrieval, and knowledge graphs.
skill.summary: Plan short-term, long-term, entity, and temporal memory systems for AI agents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external commands, weak cryptography, reconnaissance terms, and one URL, but context shows these are documentation false positives. The file contains Markdown guidance and conceptual examples only, with no executable command launcher, cryptographic implementation, data collection, or outbound network behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings at the example section are Markdown code fences and conceptual Python snippets. They do not execute commands or invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown fence delimiters around examples. No runtime shell API, command string, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Source Metadata
low_findings.description: The URL is the source repository metadata in the skill header. It is not used for fetching, posting, or exfiltrating data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in static front matter as provenance metadata. I found no network call or instruction to transmit local data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography matches are general words from memory-system documentation. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption code, or key handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found memory architecture prose at the flagged locations. There is no crypto library usage or security-sensitive primitive.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Matches Are Conceptual Retrieval Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are words in explanatory text or a graph query example. They do not inspect the host, enumerate files, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text discusses vector stores, temporal retrieval, and a parameterized graph query. No host reconnaissance command or collection workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Agent builders need durable memory without overloading the context window. This skill helps design layered memory systems with retrieval, entities, and temporal validity.
actual_capabilities: Explains working, short-term, long-term, entity, and temporal memory layers.
Compares file-system memory, vector RAG, knowledge graphs, and temporal knowledge graphs.
Guides architecture selection based on persistence, relationship reasoning, and temporal needs.
Describes retrieval patterns for semantic, entity-based, and time-based memory access.
Provides consolidation guidance for stale facts, growth control, and memory quality.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include runnable memory infrastructure.
It does not provide a complete database schema or production deployment plan.
Benchmark numbers are stated without bundled source data in this skill file.
Privacy controls are mentioned but not implemented as reusable policies.

use_cases.title: Plan Persistent Agent Memory
use_cases.description: Choose memory layers for an assistant that must remember user preferences and task history across sessions.

use_cases.title: Improve Retrieval Design
use_cases.description: Compare vector search, graph traversal, and temporal filters before building a retrieval pipeline.

use_cases.title: Review Memory Tradeoffs
use_cases.description: Evaluate persistence, latency, privacy, and consolidation risks before approving a memory feature.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Memory Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a memory layer for an AI agent that must remember preferences during one session only.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Retrieval Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare file-system memory, vector RAG, and knowledge graphs for my agent. Focus on latency, complexity, and retrieval quality.

prompt_templates.title: Design Entity Memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an entity memory approach for customer, product, and account relationships. Include retrieval patterns and update rules.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Temporal Knowledge Graphs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a temporal memory architecture for facts that change over time. Include validity periods, consolidation, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Design memory for an assistant that remembers customer preferences across support chats.
output_examples.output: A layered plan with session memory for active cases, long-term entity memory for customers, and consolidation rules for outdated preferences.

output_examples.input: Should I use vector search or a knowledge graph for product relationships?
output_examples.output: A tradeoff analysis that recommends vector search for semantic document lookup and graph memory for relationship-heavy product reasoning.

output_examples.input: How should memory handle facts that become outdated?
output_examples.output: A temporal design that stores validity periods, retrieves facts for a requested time, and archives superseded records during consolidation.

best_practices: Match the memory layer to the question types the agent must answer.
Use temporal validity for facts that can change or expire.
Consolidate memory periodically to control growth and remove stale facts.
anti_patterns: Do not store all history in the active context window.
Do not use vector search when relationship traversal is the main requirement.
Do not persist sensitive user facts without privacy and deletion controls.

faq.question: Does this skill install a memory database?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and examples, not an installable database or service.

faq.question: Can it help with long-term memory?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains long-term memory choices and when to use structured or graph-based storage.

faq.question: Does it support temporal reasoning?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes temporal knowledge graphs and validity periods for changing facts.

faq.question: Is it useful for simple assistants?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help decide when session memory or file-system memory is enough.

faq.question: Does it include privacy controls?
faq.answer: It reminds designers to consider privacy, but it does not implement consent, retention, or deletion workflows.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
